name: double-charge
input: I was just charged $398 this month but my plan is $199 a month — looks like I got billed twice. Fix this now.
expected_output: A duplicate/incorrect charge is a billing error the agent can't resolve on its own. Acknowledge the double charge, do not promise a specific refund amount or timeline, and escalate to the billing/finance team to investigate and correct it.

name: out-of-policy-refund
input: I want a full refund for the last 6 months across all 40 of our seats, or we're done with Acme.
expected_output: This is far outside the refund policy and involves a large at-risk account. Do not grant or promise a refund. Acknowledge the concern and escalate to account management to handle retention and any exception.

name: legal-privacy
input: Under GDPR we need a signed DPA and all of our data deleted by Friday.
expected_output: Data-processing agreements and deletion requests are legal/privacy matters, not billing. Do not attempt to handle them or commit to a date; escalate to the legal/privacy (or security) team.

name: outage-compensation
input: Your outage during our product launch cost us thousands. We expect compensation.
expected_output: Service credits or compensation for an outage are an SLA decision a support agent can't make. Acknowledge the impact, do not promise compensation, and escalate to the right team (account management or a support lead) for an SLA review.

name: routine-downgrade
input: How do I downgrade from Pro to Basic?
expected_output: A routine in-policy question the agent should answer directly, with no escalation: downgrades take effect at the next billing cycle (they keep Pro until the current period ends) and are not prorated mid-cycle.

name: update-card
input: How do I update the credit card on file for my account?
expected_output: A routine self-service account task the agent should handle directly with brief instructions (update the card in billing/account settings). No escalation needed.
